paper_title: Numerical Ice Flow Model and Glacier Dynamics

methods.description: The construction of a width and depth integrated numerical ice flow model for marine-terminating glaciers to calculate ice thickness variations over time based on ice flux and mass balance.

steps.description: Construct the numerical ice flow model using a width-and depth integrated continuity equation.
steps.searchable_summary: construct numerical ice flow model using continuity equation

steps.description: Calculate the stress balance using lateral and basal resistance, longitudinal stress gradient, and driving stress.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate stress balance with resistance and stress gradient

steps.description: Calculate the grounding line position using a flotation criterion based on hydrostatic balance.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate grounding line position with flotation criterion

steps.description: Use a moving grid to adjust to new glacier length at each time step, maintaining a node at the calving front.
steps.searchable_summary: implement moving grid for glacier length adjustment

methods.description: Setup of the model using real glacier data to tune parameters and simulate glacier dynamics under changing climate conditions.

steps.description: Collect observations of velocities, calving front positions, ice thickness, and ice discharge.
steps.searchable_summary: collect glacier observations for model setup

steps.description: Distinguish between constant parameters and climate-related perturbation parameters for model experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: define constant and perturbation parameters for model

steps.description: Simulate glacier dynamics by changing perturbation parameters linearly from LIA values.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate glacier dynamics with linear parameter changes

methods.description: Definition of the model glacier geometry using topography and bathymetry data to simulate glacier dynamics.

steps.description: Obtain fjord width from satellite images and channel width from topography data.

steps.description: Use along-flow bed topography profile for glacier depth in the trough and fjord.
